# MakerSquare

> MakerSquare is a 4-week, in-person AI bootcamp in Austin, TX for non-technical professionals — operators, founders, career builders, and domain experts. No coding required. Students build and deploy 3 real AI products and present at Demo Day in front of Austin's tech, investor, and operator community.

## What MakerSquare Is

MakerSquare is the only in-person AI product-building bootcamp in Austin, Texas designed specifically for professionals without a technical background. Unlike online courses or certifications that teach AI literacy, MakerSquare graduates ship working AI products. The program is 4 weeks long, meets Monday, Wednesday, and Friday from 9am to 3pm, and is intentionally kept to 15 builders per cohort.

The program is open to anyone nationwide (and internationally) — not just Austin locals. Housing assistance is available for builders who relocate.

## Who It's For

- Business operators who want to automate their workflows
- Career professionals who want to future-proof their skills with real AI building experience
- Domain experts with industry knowledge and a specific problem to solve
- First-time founders who want to build a product without hiring a developer

## What Students Build

Over 4 weeks, students build and deploy 3 AI products:
1. Week 1: First deployed AI tool — advanced prompting, Windsurf, direct API integrations
2. Week 2: RAG-powered product — AI that reasons over your own documents using Supabase
3. Week 3: Interface + voice/multimodal product — real UI with v0 and Replit, voice with ElevenLabs/Whisper
4. Week 4: Capstone — most ambitious product combining RAG, agents, interfaces. Presented at Demo Day.

## Curriculum Overview

- Pre-work: 3 self-paced steps (describe your role, pick your use case, build your first prompt system)
- Week 1 — Advanced Foundations: Advanced prompting, structured output, Windsurf, API integrations, Product 1 sprint
- Week 2 — RAG + Agents: Vector embeddings on Supabase, AI agents, automated testing with Braintrust. Product 1 ships.
- Week 3 — Interfaces + Voice: v0 and Replit frontends, ElevenLabs/Whisper, Claude Vision. Product 2 ships.
- Week 4 — Ship & Scale: Capstone scoping, full build sprint, Demo Day presentation

No visual automation tools (Zapier, Make, n8n). Production-level skills: prompting, RAG, agents, automated testing, deployment.

## Tools & Tech Stack

Claude API, ChatGPT, Windsurf, Supabase, v0, Railway, Replit, ElevenLabs, Whisper, Claude Vision, Braintrust, Airtable, Google Sheets, Notion

## Key Facts

- Duration: 4 weeks
- Format: In-person, Austin TX (Mon/Wed/Fri, 9am–3pm)
- Prerequisites: No coding required. Basic computer fluency + real-world domain expertise.
- Cohort 1: July 6 – August 1, 2026 (Demo Day Aug 1)
- Cohort 2: September 14 – October 9, 2026
- Cohort 3: October 26 – November 20, 2026
- Cohort 4: January 11 – February 5, 2027
- Cohort size: 15 builders per cohort
- Tuition: $5,999 (flat fee, no ISA)
- Payment plans: Pay in full $5,999 or 2 payments of $3,100 ($6,199 total)
- Deposit: $1,000 locks seat
- Scholarship: $1,500 off for military, first responders, and educators ($4,499 total)
- Housing: Partner Airbnb discounts from $1,600/month
- Corporate training: 2-week intensive for teams of 8–20 (custom pricing)
- Alumni subscription: $199/month (optional, first month free)

## Demo Day

Every cohort ends with a public Demo Day at MakerSquare HQ in Austin. Each builder presents their capstone AI product to an audience of seed-stage investors, Austin tech hub leaders, and operators. 5-minute live demos with real feedback from people who build and invest.

## Why AI Skills Matter Now

88% of organizations use AI in at least one business function, but only 1% have achieved real AI maturity (McKinsey, 2025). Jobs requiring AI fluency have grown 7x in two years (Gloat, 2026). 59% of enterprise leaders report an AI skills gap (DataCamp, 2026). The gap between people who prompt AI and people who build with AI is widening every month.

## Differentiators

- The only in-person AI product-building bootcamp for non-technical professionals in Austin
- Builds real, deployed products — not coursework or certificates
- Graduates ship 3 AI products and present at Demo Day to Austin investors and operators
- No visual automation tools — teaches production-level AI building with Windsurf, APIs, RAG, agents
- Schedule allows builders to keep working 2 days per week (Tue/Thu)
- 4.41/5 alumni rating on Course Report (legacy MakerSquare program)

## Founder

Marliis Schneider, Founder & CEO. Background in operations, business development, and startup ecosystems. Built MakerSquare after seeing the gap between professionals who know what AI is and those who know how to make it work. Based in Austin, TX.

## Advisory Board

Ravi Parikh — Co-founder & CEO, Heap (YC alum). Additional advisors from Austin's operator, investor, and AI community being assembled.
